slides.title: Testosterone fades slowly. *Your habits decide* how fast.
slides.sub: Levels drift down about 1 percent a year in men. How you train, sleep and recover changes the slope.

slides.title: A slow, steady drift
slides.body: After the mid-thirties, testosterone tends to decline *about 1 percent a year*. It is gradual, and much of it is shaped by daily habits.

slides.title: More than libido
slides.body: Healthy levels are linked to muscle, bone, mood and energy. Protecting them is really about *protecting vitality* as you age.

slides.title: Lift heavy things
slides.body: Resistance training is one of the clearest natural supports. A few strength sessions a week *can support healthy levels* and lean mass.

slides.title: Defend deep sleep
slides.body: Most testosterone is released during sleep. Even one week of short nights *may lower* daytime levels in healthy men.

slides.title: Mind your body fat
slides.body: Excess body fat is linked to lower testosterone. Reaching a *healthier body composition* often helps levels recover.

slides.title: Age slower, *stay strong* longer.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the sleep, movement, food and steps behind healthy aging, turning your strength and recovery habits into one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Testosterone slips about 1 percent a year, but the slope is not fixed. 💪🌿

Lift heavy, guard your deep sleep and keep a healthy body composition, and you can hold onto strength and vitality for far longer. Save this. 🔖
